goal: Redesign office TMX map with Limezu Modern Interiors 16x16 asset pack

risks: Tile ID mapping is error-prone: the builder must visually read 16x16 tileset PNGs to identify correct tile IDs for furniture items, and wrong IDs produce garbage tiles. The Limezu master sheet (Interiors_16x16.png) is massive — using themed sub-sheets reduces this risk.

tilesetCache.ts hardcodes two tilesets (room-builder firstgid=1, furniture firstgid=225). Adding a new tileset requires updating this loader with the correct firstgid and image path, otherwise tiles render as blank. This is a code change the builder must not forget.
The seats objectgroup has 22 objects with precise pixel coordinates. Any accidental edit to this section corrupts agent seating. The builder must work carefully around it, only touching tile layers.
over_engineering_flags: The directive asks for themed areas (lounge, kitchen, conference, CEO office) which could spiral into endless decoration tweaking. The builder should aim for 'clearly furnished and distinct' rather than pixel-perfect interior design.
Using the full Interiors_16x16.png master sheet would be overkill — the themed sub-sheets (Generic, Kitchen, Conference Hall, LivingRoom) are better scoped and easier to map tile IDs from.
challenges.recommendation: Proceed as-is. The scope is clear: copy themed tileset PNGs, register them in TMX + tilesetCache, populate furniture layers. Single project, single builder. Use Theme_Sorter PNGs (not the massive master sheet) for manageable tile ID mapping.

projects.title: Populate office TMX with Limezu Modern Interiors furniture
projects.scope_summary: Copy relevant Limezu 16x16 Theme_Sorter PNGs (Generic, LivingRoom, Kitchen, Conference Hall) into public/assets/office/. Create corresponding .tsx tileset reference files. Add the new tilesets to office.tmx with correct firstgid values. Update tilesetCache.ts to load the new tileset images. Then populate the furniture/decoration tile layers with rich content: plants, rugs, paintings, bookshelves, sofas in a lounge area, kitchen/break room with appliances, conference room with podium/projector screen, upgraded CEO office, and decorative items throughout open areas and hallway. Preserve Floors layer (locked) and seats objectgroup untouched.
